## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    '006' || SUBSTRING(MD5(opps.opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(opps.titel)), 'Unnamed Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(opps.vertriebsphase))
        WHEN 'in kontakt' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN opps.zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(opps.zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(opps.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TRIM(opps.zieldatum)
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(opps.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(opps.zieldatum), 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(opps.auftragswert) IS NULL OR TRIM(opps.auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(opps.auftragswert)) = 'NONE' THEN NULL
        -- European format: digits.digits,digits (e.g., "400.902,63") - dot as thousands sep, comma as decimal
        WHEN TRIM(opps.auftragswert) ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d{3},\d+$' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(opps.auftragswert), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
        -- Standard format with or without prefix (e.g., "EUR 144893.69", "116121.28")
        ELSE
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(opps.auftragswert), '[^0-9.\-]', '', 'g') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(opps.waehrungscode))
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CASE WHEN cust.kundennummer IS NOT NULL
        THEN 'A' || SUBSTRING(MD5(cust.kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15)
    END AS "AccountId",
    opps.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0::INTEGER AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} opps
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} cust
    ON REPLACE(opps.kunden_ref, 'KD-', 'CUST-') = cust.kundennummer
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opp_kennung: Deterministic Salesforce-style 15-char Id derived from MD5 of opp_kennung, prefixed with '006' (Opportunity prefix).
  - Name <- titel: Title cleaned with INITCAP and TRIM; defaults to 'Unnamed Opportunity' if missing.
  - StageName <- vertriebsphase: Maps mixed German/English sales pipeline phases to standard Salesforce stages. Case-insensitive matching with LOWER/TRIM.
  - CloseDate <- zieldatum: Parses multiple date formats (DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY) into consistent YYYY-MM-DD text. NULL if unparseable.
  - Amount <- auftragswert: Cleans numeric values: detects European format (dot-thousands-sep + comma-decimal like '400.902,63') by removing dots and swapping comma→period; handles standard format with or without EUR prefix. Returns NULL for 'None' or empty.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- waehrungscode: Normalizes inconsistent currency values ('€', '$', 'EUR', 'Euro', '$', 'Dollar', 'CHF', etc.) to ISO 4217 codes. NULL if unrecognized.
  - AccountId <- kunden_ref, master_kunden.kundennummer: Transforms KD- prefix to CUST- to join with master_kunden, then generates Salesforce-style Account Id as 'A' + MD5 hash of customer number.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opp_kennung: Direct mapping from source natural key opp_kennung (e.g., 'OPP-00001').
  - CreatedDate <- (none): No source column available; set to NULL.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): No source column available; set to NULL.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default value 0 (not deleted) — no delete flag in source data.
Reasoning: Fixed the Amount calculation by using two separate, flat CASE branches: one for European format (detected via regex `'^\-?\d+\.\d{3},\d+$'` matching patterns like "400.902,63") which removes dots and swaps comma→period; and a fallback for standard format/signed prefixes that strips non-numeric chars with a single REGEXP_REPLACE. Both branches use CAST(... AS DOUBLE PRECISION) explicitly, avoiding the nested function type-inference error from the previous attempt. Also added MM/DD/YYYY date parsing to CloseDate since sample data contains dates like "4/28/2025" and "2/16/2027".

## Project__c
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    'a2X' || MD5(p.projekt_kennung) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(p.projektname)), 'Unnamed Project') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('aktiv', 'active') THEN 'Active'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('abgeschlossen', 'completed') THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('planung', 'in planung', 'in planning') THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('pausiert', 'on hold') THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('storniert', 'cancelled') THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' AND p.go_live_datum != '0000-00-00' THEN p.go_live_datum
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    'A' || MD5(k.kundennummer) AS "Account__c",
    '006' || MD5(o.opp_kennung) AS "Opportunity__c",
    p.projekt_kennung AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} p
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} k
    ON p.kunden_kennung = k.kundennummer
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
    ON p.opp_kennung_ref = o.opp_kennung
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- projekt_kennung: Generated Salesforce-style ID as 'a2X' prefix + MD5 hash of projekt_kennung
  - Name <- projektname: INITCAP(TRIM(projektname)) with 'Unnamed Project' fallback for NULL/empty values
  - Project_Status__c <- projektstatus: Mapped German/English status values to enum (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled). Case-insensitive substring matching. NULL on unmatched.
  - Go_Live_Date__c <- go_live_datum: Parsed dates from DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, MM/DD/YYYY, or YYYYMMDD formats to ISO YYYY-MM-DD text. NULL for empty/unparseable/sentinel dates like '0000-00-00'.
  - Account__c <- kunden_kennung, master_kunden.kundennummer: Generated as 'A' || MD5(kundennummer) after JOIN with master_kunden to resolve Account Id reference
  - Opportunity__c <- opp_kennung_ref, master_opportunities.opp_kennung: Generated as '006' || MD5(opp_kennung) after JOIN with master_opportunities to resolve Opportunity Id reference. Left join handles 62 unmatched OPP-M-* refs.
  - Legacy_Project_ID__c <- projekt_kennung: Direct copy of source natural key projekt_kennung
  - CreatedDate <- (none): NULL - not present in source data
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): NULL - not present in source data
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default value 0 (false) as no deletion indicator exists in source
Reasoning: Global mapping plan for 5 target tables derived from fixture_master_v2_src source schema. Key cross-table decisions:

1. **Key Resolution Strategy**: All `AccountId` / `Account__c` fields must reference Salesforce-style Account.Id, not raw source customer numbers (kundennummer). Generate Account.Id as 'A' prefix + MD5 hash of kundennummer. Similarly, Opportunity.Id as '006' prefix + MD5 of opp_kennung, Project__c.Id as 'a2X' prefix + MD5 of projekt_kennung, Installed_Asset__c.Id as 'a1Y' prefix + MD5 of asset_kennung, Contact.Id as '003' prefix + MD5 of kontakt_id.

2. **Legacy Columns**: Every Legacy_*__c column populated from source natural key:
   - Legacy_Customer_ID__c = kundennummer (master_kunden)
   - Legacy_Contact_ID__c = kontakt_id (master_kontakte)
   - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c = opp_kennung (master_opportunities)
   - Legacy_Project_ID__c = projekt_kennung (master_projekte)
   - Legacy_Asset_ID__c = asset_kennung (master_assets)

3. **Enum Mappings** (case-insensitive, trim whitespace):
   - Customer_Tier__c: 'Gold'→Gold, 'Silber'/'Silver'→Silver, 'Bronze'/'Kupfer'→Bronze, 'Platin'/'Platinum'→Platinum; fallback NULL
   - Role__c: Maps from German/English values to (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor); NULL on unmatched
   - Preferred_Language__c: DE→DE, EN/ENG→EN, FR/FRA→FR, ES/ESP→ES, IT/ITA→IT; case-insensitive prefix match on first 2 chars, then exact match
   - StageName (Opportunity): vertriebsphase mapped to Sales Pipeline stages; NULL on unmatched
   - Project_Status__c: projektstatus mapped to (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled); NULL on unmatched

4. **Date Parsing**: All source date fields (garantieende, zieldatum, go_live_datum) accept DD.MM.YYYY format primarily; also handle YYYY-MM-DD and YYYYMMDD patterns. Use regex guards:
   - If matches `\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}` → TO_DATE(col, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
   - If matches `\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}` → col::TEXT
   - If matches `\d{8}` (pure digits) → TO_DATE(col, 'YYYYMMDD')
   - NULL on unparseable/empty

5. **Amount Handling**: auftragswert field requires:
   - Strip currency symbols (€, $, etc.) and whitespace
   - Detect European format (contains dot before comma, e.g. "1.234,56"): remove dots, replace comma with period
   - Standard numeric cleanup, then CAST TO DOUBLE PRECISION
   - NULL on empty or unparseable

6. **Key Join Chain**:
   - master_kunden.kundennummer ↔ master_opportunities.kunden_ref (Opportunity.AccountId via Account.Id)
   - master_kunden.kundennummer ↔ master_projekte.kunden_kennung (Project__c.Account__c via Account.Id)
   - master_projekte.projekt_kennung ↔ master_assets.projekt_kennung (Installed_Asset__c.Project__c via Project__c.Id)
   - master_projekte.opp_kennung_ref ↔ master_opportunities.opp_kennung (Project__c.Opportunity__c via Opportunity.Id)

7. **Normalization**: Apply INITCAP to Name fields, UPPER to currency codes, LOWER/TRIM for enum lookups. All text columns TRIM'd before lookup.
Project__c model transforms master_projekte with two LEFT JOINs: one to master_kunden for Account lookup and one to master_opportunities for Opportunity lookup. All Salesforce-style IDs are generated using the 'a2X'/'A'/'006' prefix + MD5 hash strategy. German enum values for project status are mapped case-insensitively to standard English values, and multi-format dates (DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYYMMDD) are parsed to ISO format with NULL fallbacks for invalid or sentinel dates.

Review every table and return your verdicts.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 8, 19, 4, 473471, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))], timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 8, 19, 4, 473685, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc), run_id='019f5a8e-ee98-7307-a732-4297f1056255', conversation_id='019f5a8e-ee98-7307-a732-42965d86ce97')

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    '003' || MD5(k.kontakt_id) AS "Id",
    TRIM(k.rufname) AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(k.familienname), ''), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(k.kontakt_email)) AS "Email",
    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(k.tel), '[^0-9+]', '', 'g') AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.berufsbezeichnung)) AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR', 'SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER', 'TECHNIKER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'DE' THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'EN' OR UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) = 'GERMAN' THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'FR' THEN 'FR'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'ES' THEN 'ES'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'IT' THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    a.Id AS "AccountId",
    k.kontakt_id AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
     0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kontakte') }} k
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT
        'A' || MD5(kundennummer) AS Id,
        kundennummer
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
) a ON k.kd_nummer = a.kundennummer
```

  - FirstName <- rufname: Trimmed first name; nullable, no fallback needed

  - Email <- kontakt_email: Lowercased and trimmed email address

  - Role__c <- rolle: Enum mapping: Decision Maker (Decision Maker/Entscheider), End User (End User/Endanwender), Executive Sponsor (Executive Sponsor/Sponsor), Technical Contact (Technical Contact/Technischer Ansprechpartner/Techniker); NULL for N/A or empty
  - Preferred_Language__c <- korrespondenzsprache: Language code extraction via prefix match on first 2 chars uppercase: DE, EN (incl. German), FR, ES, IT; NULL for unsupported languages

  - Legacy_Contact_ID__c <- kontakt_id: Direct copy of source natural key kontakt_id for row-level verification
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Not present in source; set to NULL
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Not present in source; set to NULL
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default value 0 (false); no deletion tracking in source data

## Installed_Asset__c
```sql
WITH asset_base AS (
    SELECT
        a.asset_kennung,
        a.asset_name,
        a.serien_nummer,
        a.garantieende,
        a.kunden_kennung,
        a.projekt_kennung
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_assets') }} a
),

-- Join customers for Account__c lookup
assets_with_account AS (
    SELECT
        ab.*,
        k.kundennummer AS matched_kundennummer
    FROM asset_base ab
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} k
        ON TRIM(ab.kunden_kennung) = TRIM(k.kundennummer)
),

-- Join projects for Project__c lookup
assets_full AS (
    SELECT
        aa.*,
        p.projekt_kennung AS matched_projekt_kennung
    FROM assets_with_account aa
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} p
        ON TRIM(aa.projekt_kennung) = TRIM(p.projekt_kennung)
),

parsed_dates AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        -- Warranty date: DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, or YYYYMMDD patterns; NULL on unparseable
        CASE
            WHEN garantieende IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(garantieende, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN garantieende
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(garantieende, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS parsed_warranty_date
    FROM assets_full
)

SELECT
    -- Id: Salesforce-style ID generated from asset_kennung natural key
    'a1Y' || MD5(asset_kennung) AS "Id",

    -- Name: INITCAP of trimmed asset_name; fallback for empty/null
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)), 'Unnamed Asset') AS "Name",

    -- Serial_Number__c: trimmed, NULL allowed
    TRIM(serien_nummer) AS "Serial_Number__c",

    -- Warranty_End_Date__c: parsed date or NULL
    parsed_warranty_date AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",

    -- Account__c: Salesforce Account ID via matched kundennummer → 'A' || MD5
    CASE
        WHEN matched_kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN 'A' || MD5(matched_kundennummer)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Account__c",

    -- Project__c: Salesforce Project ID via matched projekt_kennung → 'a2X' || MD5
    CASE
        WHEN matched_projekt_kennung IS NOT NULL THEN 'a2X' || MD5(matched_projekt_kennung)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project__c",

    -- Legacy_Asset_ID__c: natural key preserved
    asset_kennung AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",

    -- CreatedDate / LastModifiedDate: not in source, set NULL
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",

    -- IsDeleted: default 0 (false)
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM parsed_dates
```

  - Name <- asset_name: INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)) with 'Unnamed Asset' fallback for null/empty values to satisfy NOT NULL constraint
  - Serial_Number__c <- serien_nummer: TRIM(serien_nummer) directly from source; NULL allowed per target spec

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    '006' || SUBSTRING(MD5(opps.opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(opps.titel)), 'Unnamed Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(opps.vertriebsphase))
        WHEN 'in kontakt' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN opps.zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(opps.zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(opps.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TRIM(opps.zieldatum)
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(opps.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(opps.zieldatum), 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(opps.auftragswert) IS NULL OR TRIM(opps.auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(opps.auftragswert)) = 'NONE' THEN NULL
        -- European format: digits.digits,digits (e.g., "400.902,63") - dot as thousands sep, comma as decimal
        WHEN TRIM(opps.auftragswert) ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d{3},\d+$' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(opps.auftragswert), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
        -- Standard format with or without prefix (e.g., "EUR 144893.69", "116121.28")
        ELSE
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(opps.auftragswert), '[^0-9.\-]', '', 'g') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(opps.waehrungscode))
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CASE WHEN cust.kundennummer IS NOT NULL
        THEN 'A' || SUBSTRING(MD5(cust.kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15)
    END AS "AccountId",
    opps.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0::INTEGER AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} opps
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} cust
    ON REPLACE(opps.kunden_ref, 'KD-', 'CUST-') = cust.kundennummer
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opp_kennung: Deterministic Salesforce-style 15-char Id derived from MD5 of opp_kennung, prefixed with '006' (Opportunity prefix).
  - Name <- titel: Title cleaned with INITCAP and TRIM; defaults to 'Unnamed Opportunity' if missing.
  - StageName <- vertriebsphase: Maps mixed German/English sales pipeline phases to standard Salesforce stages. Case-insensitive matching with LOWER/TRIM.
  - CloseDate <- zieldatum: Parses multiple date formats (DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY) into consistent YYYY-MM-DD text. NULL if unparseable.
  - Amount <- auftragswert: Cleans numeric values: detects European format (dot-thousands-sep + comma-decimal like '400.902,63') by removing dots and swapping comma→period; handles standard format with or without EUR prefix. Returns NULL for 'None' or empty.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- waehrungscode: Normalizes inconsistent currency values ('€', '$', 'EUR', 'Euro', '$', 'Dollar', 'CHF', etc.) to ISO 4217 codes. NULL if unrecognized.
  - AccountId <- kunden_ref, master_kunden.kundennummer: Transforms KD- prefix to CUST- to join with master_kunden, then generates Salesforce-style Account Id as 'A' + MD5 hash of customer number.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opp_kennung: Direct mapping from source natural key opp_kennung (e.g., 'OPP-00001').
  - CreatedDate <- (none): No source column available; set to NULL.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): No source column available; set to NULL.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default value 0 (not deleted) — no delete flag in source data.
Reasoning: Fixed the Amount calculation by using two separate, flat CASE branches: one for European format (detected via regex `'^\-?\d+\.\d{3},\d+$'` matching patterns like "400.902,63") which removes dots and swaps comma→period; and a fallback for standard format/signed prefixes that strips non-numeric chars with a single REGEXP_REPLACE. Both branches use CAST(... AS DOUBLE PRECISION) explicitly, avoiding the nested function type-inference error from the previous attempt. Also added MM/DD/YYYY date parsing to CloseDate since sample data contains dates like "4/28/2025" and "2/16/2027".

## Project__c
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    'a2X' || MD5(p.projekt_kennung) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(p.projektname)), 'Unnamed Project') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('aktiv', 'active') THEN 'Active'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('abgeschlossen', 'completed') THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('planung', 'in planung', 'in planning') THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('pausiert', 'on hold') THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('storniert', 'cancelled') THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' AND p.go_live_datum != '0000-00-00' THEN p.go_live_datum
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    'A' || MD5(k.kundennummer) AS "Account__c",
    '006' || MD5(o.opp_kennung) AS "Opportunity__c",
    p.projekt_kennung AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} p
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} k
    ON p.kunden_kennung = k.kundennummer
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} o
    ON p.opp_kennung_ref = o.opp_kennung

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 13, 8, 19, 4, 473465, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Output schema for dbt model results: `r5zm3v23z_plan_write_critique_master_v2`
Output tables to inspect (quoted exactly): "r5zm3v23z_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Account", "r5zm3v23z_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Contact", "r5zm3v23z_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Opportunity", "r5zm3v23z_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Project__c", "r5zm3v23z_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Installed_Asset__c".
Use query_postgres to inspect tables in that schema after dbt has run.

# Proposed mapping

## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    'A' || MD5(kundennummer) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(unternehmensname)) AS "Name",
    TRIM(erp_nr) AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('GOLD') THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('SILBER', 'SILVER') THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('BRONZE', 'KUPFER') THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('PLATIN', 'PLATINUM') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(industrie)) AS "Industry",
    TRIM(homepage) AS "Website",
    INITCAP(TRIM(stadt)) AS "BillingCity",
    TRIM(land_region) AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- kundennummer: Salesforce-style Id generated as 'A' prefix + MD5 hash of the natural key kundennummer
  - Name <- unternehmensname: INITCAP(TRIM(unternehmensname)) for consistent capitalization
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_nr: Direct TRIM of source erp_nr field
  - Customer_Tier__c <- kundenklasse: Case-insensitive enum mapping: Gold→Gold, Silber/Silver→Silver, Bronze/Kupfer→Bronze, Platin/Platinum→Platinum; NULL on unmatched
  - Region__c <- vertriebsgebiet: TRIM of source vertriebsgebiet (sales region)
  - Industry <- industrie: INITCAP(TRIM(industrie)) for consistent capitalization
  - Website <- homepage: TRIM of source homepage URL field
  - BillingCity <- stadt: INITCAP(TRIM(stadt)) for city name normalization
  - BillingCountry <- land_region: TRIM of land_region (country/region)
  - Legacy_Customer_ID__c <- kundennummer: Natural key kundennummer copied as-is for row-level verification
  - CreatedDate <- (none): No source equivalent — set to NULL
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): No source equivalent — set to NULL
  - IsDeleted <- (none): No deletion tracking in source — default 0 (false)
Reasoning: Global mapping plan for 5 target tables derived from fixture_master_v2_src source schema. Key cross-table decisions:

1. **Key Resolution Strategy**: All `AccountId` / `Account__c` fields must reference Salesforce-style Account.Id, not raw source customer numbers (kundennummer). Generate Account.Id as 'A' prefix + MD5 hash of kundennummer. Similarly, Opportunity.Id as '006' prefix + MD5 of opp_kennung, Project__c.Id as 'a2X' prefix + MD5 of projekt_kennung, Installed_Asset__c.Id as 'a1Y' prefix + MD5 of asset_kennung, Contact.Id as '003' prefix + MD5 of kontakt_id.

2. **Legacy Columns**: Every Legacy_*__c column populated from source natural key:
   - Legacy_Customer_ID__c = kundennummer (master_kunden)
   - Legacy_Contact_ID__c = kontakt_id (master_kontakte)
   - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c = opp_kennung (master_opportunities)
   - Legacy_Project_ID__c = projekt_kennung (master_projekte)
   - Legacy_Asset_ID__c = asset_kennung (master_assets)

3. **Enum Mappings** (case-insensitive, trim whitespace):
   - Customer_Tier__c: 'Gold'→Gold, 'Silber'/'Silver'→Silver, 'Bronze'/'Kupfer'→Bronze, 'Platin'/'Platinum'→Platinum; fallback NULL
   - Role__c: Maps from German/English values to (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor); NULL on unmatched

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    '003' || MD5(k.kontakt_id) AS "Id",
    TRIM(k.rufname) AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(k.familienname), ''), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(k.kontakt_email)) AS "Email",
    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(k.tel), '[^0-9+]', '', 'g') AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.berufsbezeichnung)) AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR', 'SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER', 'TECHNIKER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'DE' THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'EN' OR UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) = 'GERMAN' THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'FR' THEN 'FR'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'ES' THEN 'ES'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'IT' THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    a.Id AS "AccountId",
    k.kontakt_id AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
     0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kontakte') }} k
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT
        'A' || MD5(kundennummer) AS Id,
        kundennummer
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
) a ON k.kd_nummer = a.kundennummer

## Installed_Asset__c
```sql
WITH asset_base AS (
    SELECT
        a.asset_kennung,
        a.asset_name,
        a.serien_nummer,
        a.garantieende,
        a.kunden_kennung,
        a.projekt_kennung
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_assets') }} a
),

-- Join customers for Account__c lookup
assets_with_account AS (
    SELECT
        ab.*,
        k.kundennummer AS matched_kundennummer
    FROM asset_base ab
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} k
        ON TRIM(ab.kunden_kennung) = TRIM(k.kundennummer)
),

-- Join projects for Project__c lookup
assets_full AS (
    SELECT
        aa.*,
        p.projekt_kennung AS matched_projekt_kennung
    FROM assets_with_account aa
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} p
        ON TRIM(aa.projekt_kennung) = TRIM(p.projekt_kennung)
),

parsed_dates AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        -- Warranty date: DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, or YYYYMMDD patterns; NULL on unparseable
        CASE
            WHEN garantieende IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(garantieende, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN garantieende
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(garantieende, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS parsed_warranty_date
    FROM assets_full
)

SELECT
    -- Id: Salesforce-style ID generated from asset_kennung natural key
    'a1Y' || MD5(asset_kennung) AS "Id",

    -- Name: INITCAP of trimmed asset_name; fallback for empty/null
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)), 'Unnamed Asset') AS "Name",

    -- Serial_Number__c: trimmed, NULL allowed
    TRIM(serien_nummer) AS "Serial_Number__c",

    -- Warranty_End_Date__c: parsed date or NULL
    parsed_warranty_date AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",

    -- Account__c: Salesforce Account ID via matched kundennummer → 'A' || MD5
    CASE
        WHEN matched_kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN 'A' || MD5(matched_kundennummer)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Account__c",

    -- Project__c: Salesforce Project ID via matched projekt_kennung → 'a2X' || MD5
    CASE
        WHEN matched_projekt_kennung IS NOT NULL THEN 'a2X' || MD5(matched_projekt_kennung)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project__c",

    -- Legacy_Asset_ID__c: natural key preserved
    asset_kennung AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",

    -- CreatedDate / LastModifiedDate: not in source, set NULL
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",

    -- IsDeleted: default 0 (false)
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM parsed_dates
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- asset_kennung: Salesforce-style ID generated as 'a1Y' prefix + MD5 hash of asset_kennung natural key
  - Name <- asset_name: INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)) with 'Unnamed Asset' fallback for null/empty values to satisfy NOT NULL constraint
  - Serial_Number__c <- serien_nummer: TRIM(serien_nummer) directly from source; NULL allowed per target spec
  - Warranty_End_Date__c <- garantieende: Date parsing handles DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, and YYYYMMDD patterns via TO_DATE regex guards; NULL on unparseable (e.g. 'N/A')

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    '006' || SUBSTRING(MD5(opps.opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(opps.titel)), 'Unnamed Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(opps.vertriebsphase))
        WHEN 'in kontakt' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN opps.zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(opps.zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(opps.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TRIM(opps.zieldatum)
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(opps.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(opps.zieldatum), 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(opps.auftragswert) IS NULL OR TRIM(opps.auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(opps.auftragswert)) = 'NONE' THEN NULL
        -- European format: digits.digits,digits (e.g., "400.902,63") - dot as thousands sep, comma as decimal
        WHEN TRIM(opps.auftragswert) ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d{3},\d+$' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(opps.auftragswert), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
        -- Standard format with or without prefix (e.g., "EUR 144893.69", "116121.28")
        ELSE
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(opps.auftragswert), '[^0-9.\-]', '', 'g') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(opps.waehrungscode))
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CASE WHEN cust.kundennummer IS NOT NULL
        THEN 'A' || SUBSTRING(MD5(cust.kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15)
    END AS "AccountId",
    opps.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0::INTEGER AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} opps
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} cust
    ON REPLACE(opps.kunden_ref, 'KD-', 'CUST-') = cust.kundennummer
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opp_kennung: Deterministic Salesforce-style 15-char Id derived from MD5 of opp_kennung, prefixed with '006' (Opportunity prefix).
  - Name <- titel: Title cleaned with INITCAP and TRIM; defaults to 'Unnamed Opportunity' if missing.
  - StageName <- vertriebsphase: Maps mixed German/English sales pipeline phases to standard Salesforce stages. Case-insensitive matching with LOWER/TRIM.
  - CloseDate <- zieldatum: Parses multiple date formats (DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY) into consistent YYYY-MM-DD text. NULL if unparseable.
  - Amount <- auftragswert: Cleans numeric values: detects European format (dot-thousands-sep + comma-decimal like '400.902,63') by removing dots and swapping comma→period; handles standard format with or without EUR prefix. Returns NULL for 'None' or empty.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- waehrungscode: Normalizes inconsistent currency values ('€', '$', 'EUR', 'Euro', '$', 'Dollar', 'CHF', etc.) to ISO 4217 codes. NULL if unrecognized.
  - AccountId <- kunden_ref, master_kunden.kundennummer: Transforms KD- prefix to CUST- to join with master_kunden, then generates Salesforce-style Account Id as 'A' + MD5 hash of customer number.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opp_kennung: Direct mapping from source natural key opp_kennung (e.g., 'OPP-00001').
  - CreatedDate <- (none): No source column available; set to NULL.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): No source column available; set to NULL.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default value 0 (not deleted) — no delete flag in source data.
Reasoning: Fixed the Amount calculation by using two separate, flat CASE branches: one for European format (detected via regex `'^\-?\d+\.\d{3},\d+$'` matching patterns like "400.902,63") which removes dots and swaps comma→period; and a fallback for standard format/signed prefixes that strips non-numeric chars with a single REGEXP_REPLACE. Both branches use CAST(... AS DOUBLE PRECISION) explicitly, avoiding the nested function type-inference error from the previous attempt. Also added MM/DD/YYYY date parsing to CloseDate since sample data contains dates like "4/28/2025" and "2/16/2027".

## Project__c
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    'a2X' || MD5(p.projekt_kennung) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(p.projektname)), 'Unnamed Project') AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('aktiv', 'active') THEN 'Active'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('abgeschlossen', 'completed') THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('planung', 'in planung', 'in planning') THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('pausiert', 'on hold') THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(p.projektstatus)) IN ('storniert', 'cancelled') THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' AND p.go_live_datum != '0000-00-00' THEN p.go_live_datum

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

# Proposed mapping

## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    'A' || MD5(kundennummer) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(unternehmensname)) AS "Name",
    TRIM(erp_nr) AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('GOLD') THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('SILBER', 'SILVER') THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('BRONZE', 'KUPFER') THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('PLATIN', 'PLATINUM') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(industrie)) AS "Industry",
    TRIM(homepage) AS "Website",
    INITCAP(TRIM(stadt)) AS "BillingCity",
    TRIM(land_region) AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- kundennummer: Salesforce-style Id generated as 'A' prefix + MD5 hash of the natural key kundennummer
  - Name <- unternehmensname: INITCAP(TRIM(unternehmensname)) for consistent capitalization
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_nr: Direct TRIM of source erp_nr field
  - Customer_Tier__c <- kundenklasse: Case-insensitive enum mapping: Gold→Gold, Silber/Silver→Silver, Bronze/Kupfer→Bronze, Platin/Platinum→Platinum; NULL on unmatched
  - Region__c <- vertriebsgebiet: TRIM of source vertriebsgebiet (sales region)
  - Industry <- industrie: INITCAP(TRIM(industrie)) for consistent capitalization
  - Website <- homepage: TRIM of source homepage URL field
  - BillingCity <- stadt: INITCAP(TRIM(stadt)) for city name normalization
  - BillingCountry <- land_region: TRIM of land_region (country/region)
  - Legacy_Customer_ID__c <- kundennummer: Natural key kundennummer copied as-is for row-level verification
  - CreatedDate <- (none): No source equivalent — set to NULL
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): No source equivalent — set to NULL
  - IsDeleted <- (none): No deletion tracking in source — default 0 (false)

1. **Key Resolution Strategy**: All `AccountId` / `Account__c` fields must reference Salesforce-style Account.Id, not raw source customer numbers (kundennummer). Generate Account.Id as 'A' prefix + MD5 hash of kundennummer. Similarly, Opportunity.Id as '006' prefix + MD5 of opp_kennung, Project__c.Id as 'a2X' prefix + MD5 of projekt_kennung, Installed_Asset__c.Id as 'a1Y' prefix + MD5 of asset_kennung, Contact.Id as '003' prefix + MD5 of kontakt_id.

2. **Legacy Columns**: Every Legacy_*__c column populated from source natural key:
   - Legacy_Customer_ID__c = kundennummer (master_kunden)
   - Legacy_Contact_ID__c = kontakt_id (master_kontakte)
   - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c = opp_kennung (master_opportunities)
   - Legacy_Project_ID__c = projekt_kennung (master_projekte)
   - Legacy_Asset_ID__c = asset_kennung (master_assets)

3. **Enum Mappings** (case-insensitive, trim whitespace):
   - Customer_Tier__c: 'Gold'→Gold, 'Silber'/'Silver'→Silver, 'Bronze'/'Kupfer'→Bronze, 'Platin'/'Platinum'→Platinum; fallback NULL
   - Role__c: Maps from German/English values to (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor); NULL on unmatched
   - Preferred_Language__c: DE→DE, EN/ENG→EN, FR/FRA→FR, ES/ESP→ES, IT/ITA→IT; case-insensitive prefix match on first 2 chars, then exact match
   - StageName (Opportunity): vertriebsphase mapped to Sales Pipeline stages; NULL on unmatched
   - Project_Status__c: projektstatus mapped to (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled); NULL on unmatched

4. **Date Parsing**: All source date fields (garantieende, zieldatum, go_live_datum) accept DD.MM.YYYY format primarily; also handle YYYY-MM-DD and YYYYMMDD patterns. Use regex guards:
   - If matches `\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}` → TO_DATE(col, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
   - If matches `\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}` → col::TEXT
   - If matches `\d{8}` (pure digits) → TO_DATE(col, 'YYYYMMDD')
   - NULL on unparseable/empty

5. **Amount Handling**: auftragswert field requires:
   - Strip currency symbols (€, $, etc.) and whitespace
   - Detect European format (contains dot before comma, e.g. "1.234,56"): remove dots, replace comma with period
   - Standard numeric cleanup, then CAST TO DOUBLE PRECISION
   - NULL on empty or unparseable

6. **Key Join Chain**:
   - master_kunden.kundennummer ↔ master_opportunities.kunden_ref (Opportunity.AccountId via Account.Id)
   - master_kunden.kundennummer ↔ master_projekte.kunden_kennung (Project__c.Account__c via Account.Id)
   - master_projekte.projekt_kennung ↔ master_assets.projekt_kennung (Installed_Asset__c.Project__c via Project__c.Id)
   - master_projekte.opp_kennung_ref ↔ master_opportunities.opp_kennung (Project__c.Opportunity__c via Opportunity.Id)

7. **Normalization**: Apply INITCAP to Name fields, UPPER to currency codes, LOWER/TRIM for enum lookups. All text columns TRIM'd before lookup.
Account table is built directly from master_kunden with Salesforce-style Id generation ('A' + MD5 hash). All enum values are normalized with UPPER/TRIM before case-insensitive matching to handle German variants (Silber→Silver, Platin→Platinum, etc.). Legacy columns preserve the original kundennummer natural key for downstream join verification.

## Contact
```sql

SELECT
    '003' || MD5(k.kontakt_id) AS "Id",

    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(k.tel), '[^0-9+]', '', 'g') AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.berufsbezeichnung)) AS "Title",

        ELSE NULL

    CASE

        ON TRIM(aa.projekt_kennung) = TRIM(p.projekt_kennung)
),

parsed_dates AS (

        *,
        -- Warranty date: DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, or YYYYMMDD patterns; NULL on unparseable
        CASE

            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN garantieende

    -- CreatedDate / LastModifiedDate: not in source, set NULL

FROM parsed_dates

## Opportunity
```sql

SELECT
    '006' || SUBSTRING(MD5(opps.opp_kennung) FROM 1 FOR 15) AS "Id",
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(opps.titel)), 'Unnamed Opportunity') AS "Name",
    CASE LOWER(TRIM(opps.vertriebsphase))
        WHEN 'in kontakt' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospecting' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'prospect' THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN 'qualification' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'quali' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'qualifikation' THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN 'in prüfung' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'needs analysis' THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN 'value proposition' THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN 'id. decision makers' THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN 'perception analysis' THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN 'proposal/price quote' THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN 'negotiation/review' THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN 'closed won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'gewonnen' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'won' THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN 'closed lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'verloren' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'lost' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        WHEN 'abgeschlossen (verloren)' THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN opps.zieldatum IS NULL OR TRIM(opps.zieldatum) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(opps.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN TRIM(opps.zieldatum)
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(opps.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN opps.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(TRIM(opps.zieldatum), 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(opps.auftragswert) IS NULL OR TRIM(opps.auftragswert) = '' THEN NULL
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(opps.auftragswert)) = 'NONE' THEN NULL
        -- European format: digits.digits,digits (e.g., "400.902,63") - dot as thousands sep, comma as decimal
        WHEN TRIM(opps.auftragswert) ~ '^\-?\d+\.\d{3},\d+$' THEN
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(REPLACE(TRIM(opps.auftragswert), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
        -- Standard format with or without prefix (e.g., "EUR 144893.69", "116121.28")
        ELSE
            CAST(
                REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(opps.auftragswert), '[^0-9.\-]', '', 'g') AS DOUBLE PRECISION
            )
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE UPPER(TRIM(opps.waehrungscode))
        WHEN '€' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN '$' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN '£' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'EURO' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'DOLLAR' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'EUR' THEN 'EUR'
        WHEN 'USD' THEN 'USD'
        WHEN 'GBP' THEN 'GBP'
        WHEN 'CHF' THEN 'CHF'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CASE WHEN cust.kundennummer IS NOT NULL
        THEN 'A' || SUBSTRING(MD5(cust.kundennummer) FROM 1 FOR 15)
    END AS "AccountId",
    opps.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    NULL::TEXT AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL::TEXT AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0::INTEGER AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} opps
LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} cust
    ON REPLACE(opps.kunden_ref, 'KD-', 'CUST-') = cust.kundennummer
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- opp_kennung: Deterministic Salesforce-style 15-char Id derived from MD5 of opp_kennung, prefixed with '006' (Opportunity prefix).
  - Name <- titel: Title cleaned with INITCAP and TRIM; defaults to 'Unnamed Opportunity' if missing.
  - StageName <- vertriebsphase: Maps mixed German/English sales pipeline phases to standard Salesforce stages. Case-insensitive matching with LOWER/TRIM.
  - CloseDate <- zieldatum: Parses multiple date formats (DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY) into consistent YYYY-MM-DD text. NULL if unparseable.
  - Amount <- auftragswert: Cleans numeric values: detects European format (dot-thousands-sep + comma-decimal like '400.902,63') by removing dots and swapping comma→period; handles standard format with or without EUR prefix. Returns NULL for 'None' or empty.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- waehrungscode: Normalizes inconsistent currency values ('€', '$', 'EUR', 'Euro', '$', 'Dollar', 'CHF', etc.) to ISO 4217 codes. NULL if unrecognized.
  - AccountId <- kunden_ref, master_kunden.kundennummer: Transforms KD- prefix to CUST- to join with master_kunden, then generates Salesforce-style Account Id as 'A' + MD5 hash of customer number.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- opp_kennung: Direct mapping from source natural key opp_kennung (e.g., 'OPP-00001').
  - CreatedDate <- (none): No source column available; set to NULL.

  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default value 0 (not deleted) — no delete flag in source data.

        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' AND p.go_live_datum != '0000-00-00' THEN p.go_live_datum
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
        WHEN p.go_live_datum ~ '^[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{1,2}/[0-9]{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(p.go_live_datum, 'MM/DD/YYYY')::TEXT
        ELSE NULL

    'A' || MD5(k.kundennummer) AS "Account__c",
    '006' || MD5(o.opp_kennung) AS "Opportunity__c",

messages.raw:
ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.

        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('BRONZE', 'KUPFER') THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('PLATIN', 'PLATINUM') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(industrie)) AS "Industry",
    TRIM(homepage) AS "Website",
    INITCAP(TRIM(stadt)) AS "BillingCity",
    TRIM(land_region) AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
```

1. **Key Resolution Strategy**: All `AccountId` / `Account__c` fields must reference Salesforce-style Account.Id, not raw source customer numbers (kundennummer). Generate Account.Id as 'A' prefix + MD5 hash of kundennummer. Similarly, Opportunity.Id as '006' prefix + MD5 of opp_kennung, Project__c.Id as 'a2X' prefix + MD5 of projekt_kennung, Installed_Asset__c.Id as 'a1Y' prefix + MD5 of asset_kennung, Contact.Id as '003' prefix + MD5 of kontakt_id.

   - Legacy_Project_ID__c = projekt_kennung (master_projekte)
   - Legacy_Asset_ID__c = asset_kennung (master_assets)

3. **Enum Mappings** (case-insensitive, trim whitespace):
   - Customer_Tier__c: 'Gold'→Gold, 'Silber'/'Silver'→Silver, 'Bronze'/'Kupfer'→Bronze, 'Platin'/'Platinum'→Platinum; fallback NULL
   - Role__c: Maps from German/English values to (Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor); NULL on unmatched

   - StageName (Opportunity): vertriebsphase mapped to Sales Pipeline stages; NULL on unmatched

4. **Date Parsing**: All source date fields (garantieende, zieldatum, go_live_datum) accept DD.MM.YYYY format primarily; also handle YYYY-MM-DD and YYYYMMDD patterns. Use regex guards:
   - If matches `\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}` → TO_DATE(col, 'DD.MM.YYYY')
   - If matches `\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}` → col::TEXT
   - If matches `\d{8}` (pure digits) → TO_DATE(col, 'YYYYMMDD')
   - NULL on unparseable/empty

   - Detect European format (contains dot before comma, e.g. "1.234,56"): remove dots, replace comma with period
   - Standard numeric cleanup, then CAST TO DOUBLE PRECISION
   - NULL on empty or unparseable

6. **Key Join Chain**:
   - master_kunden.kundennummer ↔ master_opportunities.kunden_ref (Opportunity.AccountId via Account.Id)
   - master_kunden.kundennummer ↔ master_projekte.kunden_kennung (Project__c.Account__c via Account.Id)

7. **Normalization**: Apply INITCAP to Name fields, UPPER to currency codes, LOWER/TRIM for enum lookups. All text columns TRIM'd before lookup.

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    '003' || MD5(k.kontakt_id) AS "Id",
    TRIM(k.rufname) AS "FirstName",
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(k.familienname), ''), 'Unknown') AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(k.kontakt_email)) AS "Email",
    REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(k.tel), '[^0-9+]', '', 'g') AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.berufsbezeichnung)) AS "Title",
    CASE
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR', 'SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER', 'TECHNIKER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'DE' THEN 'DE'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'EN' OR UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) = 'GERMAN' THEN 'EN'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'FR' THEN 'FR'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'ES' THEN 'ES'
        WHEN LEFT(UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)), 2) = 'IT' THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    a.Id AS "AccountId",
    k.kontakt_id AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",
     0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kontakte') }} k
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT
        'A' || MD5(kundennummer) AS Id,
        kundennummer
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
) a ON k.kd_nummer = a.kundennummer
```

  - Id <- kontakt_id: Salesforce-style contact ID: '003' prefix + MD5 hash of kontakt_id
  - FirstName <- rufname: Trimmed first name; nullable, no fallback needed
  - LastName <- familienname: INITCAP + trimmed last name; defaults to 'Unknown' if empty/null to satisfy NOT NULL constraint
  - Email <- kontakt_email: Lowercased and trimmed email address
  - Phone <- tel: TRIM'd and cleaned: stripped all non-numeric characters except '+' for international format
  - Title <- berufsbezeichnung: INITCAP + trimmed job title/profession from German source data
  - Role__c <- rolle: Enum mapping: Decision Maker (Decision Maker/Entscheider), End User (End User/Endanwender), Executive Sponsor (Executive Sponsor/Sponsor), Technical Contact (Technical Contact/Technischer Ansprechpartner/Techniker); NULL for N/A or empty
  - Preferred_Language__c <- korrespondenzsprache: Language code extraction via prefix match on first 2 chars uppercase: DE, EN (incl. German), FR, ES, IT; NULL for unsupported languages
  - AccountId <- kd_nummer, master_kunden.kundennummer: Joined master_kontakte.kd_nummer to master_kunden.kundennummer; output is 'A' prefix + MD5 of kundennummer (Salesforce Account Id format); NULL when customer not found
  - Legacy_Contact_ID__c <- kontakt_id: Direct copy of source natural key kontakt_id for row-level verification
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Not present in source; set to NULL
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Not present in source; set to NULL
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default value 0 (false); no deletion tracking in source data

## Installed_Asset__c
```sql
WITH asset_base AS (
    SELECT
        a.asset_kennung,
        a.asset_name,
        a.serien_nummer,
        a.garantieende,
        a.kunden_kennung,
        a.projekt_kennung
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_assets') }} a
),

-- Join customers for Account__c lookup
assets_with_account AS (
    SELECT
        ab.*,
        k.kundennummer AS matched_kundennummer
    FROM asset_base ab
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} k
        ON TRIM(ab.kunden_kennung) = TRIM(k.kundennummer)
),

-- Join projects for Project__c lookup
assets_full AS (
    SELECT
        aa.*,
        p.projekt_kennung AS matched_projekt_kennung
    FROM assets_with_account aa
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} p
        ON TRIM(aa.projekt_kennung) = TRIM(p.projekt_kennung)
),

parsed_dates AS (
    SELECT
        *,
        -- Warranty date: DD.MM.YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD, or YYYYMMDD patterns; NULL on unparseable
        CASE
            WHEN garantieende IS NULL THEN NULL
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_DATE(garantieende, 'DD.MM.YYYY')::TEXT
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN garantieende
            WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_DATE(garantieende, 'YYYYMMDD')::TEXT
            ELSE NULL
        END AS parsed_warranty_date
    FROM assets_full
)

SELECT
    -- Id: Salesforce-style ID generated from asset_kennung natural key
    'a1Y' || MD5(asset_kennung) AS "Id",

    -- Name: INITCAP of trimmed asset_name; fallback for empty/null
    COALESCE(INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)), 'Unnamed Asset') AS "Name",

    -- Serial_Number__c: trimmed, NULL allowed
    TRIM(serien_nummer) AS "Serial_Number__c",

    -- Warranty_End_Date__c: parsed date or NULL
    parsed_warranty_date AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",

    -- Account__c: Salesforce Account ID via matched kundennummer → 'A' || MD5
    CASE
        WHEN matched_kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN 'A' || MD5(matched_kundennummer)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Account__c",

    -- Project__c: Salesforce Project ID via matched projekt_kennung → 'a2X' || MD5
    CASE
        WHEN matched_projekt_kennung IS NOT NULL THEN 'a2X' || MD5(matched_projekt_kennung)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project__c",

    -- Legacy_Asset_ID__c: natural key preserved
    asset_kennung AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",

    -- CreatedDate / LastModifiedDate: not in source, set NULL
    NULL AS "CreatedDate",
    NULL AS "LastModifiedDate",

    -- IsDeleted: default 0 (false)
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM parsed_dates
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- asset_kennung: Salesforce-style ID generated as 'a1Y' prefix + MD5 hash of asset_kennung natural key
  - Name <- asset_name: INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)) with 'Unnamed Asset' fallback for null/empty values to satisfy NOT NULL constraint
  - Serial_Number__c <- serien_nummer: TRIM(serien_nummer) directly from source; NULL allowed per target spec
